codeReview.summary: The mcp-tool-annotations feature is fully implemented and working correctly. All expected behaviors are delivered: (1) ToolBehavioralHints struct in swell-core with readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, idempotentHint; (2) All built-in tools implement behavioral_hints() with appropriate values; (3) McpToolAnnotations in mcp.rs with helper methods; (4) risk_level_from_annotations() for automatic risk classification; (5) McpToolWrapper uses annotations for permission_tier() decisions (ReadOnly→Auto, Destructive→Deny); (6) Cedar policy engine has ReadOnly/Destructive ToolOperation variants; (7) 9 annotation tests pass.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed all documented conventions from AGENTS.md: used crate-scoped validation, ran cargo build --workspace for verification, ran cargo test -p swell-tools for tests, ran cargo clippy -p swell-tools for linting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows cargo build --workspace, cargo test -p swell-tools -- tool_annotation, cargo clippy -p swell-tools

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used the Rust skill properly and followed the documented procedure from the skill file. No deviations from the expected workflow.
Worker did not start any additional services beyond the standard cargo commands. No services.yaml gaps identified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows only standard cargo build/test/clippy commands

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that the feature was already partially implemented from a prior session and correctly identified what was complete vs what needed to be added. This is documented in the handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff salientSummary confirms feature was already fully implemented

summary: Feature mcp-tool-annotations passes review. Implementation is complete with ToolBehavioralHints in swell-core, McpToolAnnotations in mcp.rs, behavioral_hints() on all built-in tools, risk classification via risk_level_from_annotations(), and permission_tier() integration with ReadOnly/Destructive ToolOperation variants in Cedar policy. All 9 annotation tests pass. No blocking issues found.
